sourceRows.Title: The dressing field method of gauge symmetry reduction, a review with examples
sourceRows.Summary: Reviews the dressing-field method as a way to construct composite variables invariant under a gauge subgroup and reduce gauge symmetry without mere gauge fixing.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Strong observed-variable template; it does not derive GU-native fields, a GU vacuum, W/Z/H source rows, or masses.

sourceRows.Title: Dressing vs. Fixing: On How to Extract and Interpret Gauge-Invariant Content
sourceRows.Summary: Clarifies the difference between gauge fixing and dressing transformations, with electroweak examples where gauge-invariant variables carry physical interpretation.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Useful conceptual guardrail for observed-field extraction; still external to GU and not a numerical source law.

sourceRows.Title: Gauge-fixing in quantum field theory and the dressing field method
sourceRows.Summary: Frames dressing as a distinct operation from gauge fixing in quantum field theory and emphasizes that dressed variables can encode gauge-invariant content.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Supports an extraction discipline, not GU-local pole equations or source-lineage parameters.

sourceRows.Title: Gauge invariant accounts of the Higgs mechanism
sourceRows.Summary: Compares gauge-invariant descriptions of the Higgs mechanism and explains how physical descriptions can avoid treating gauge symmetry breaking as literal observable breaking.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Good boundary source for interpretation; it does not supply GU electroweak source rows or a mass scale.

sourceRows.Title: Second order Standard Model
sourceRows.Summary: Rewrites the electroweak theory using SU(2)-invariant fields after combining spinors and bosons with the Higgs field, leaving residual U(1) structure and Standard Model inputs.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Closest direct electroweak variable construction; it is an SM reformulation, not a GU-derived W/Z/H mass prediction.

decision: Do not promote W/Z or Higgs physical masses from dressing-field or SU(2)-invariant electroweak-variable routes in this repository. These sources provide a strong external template for observed-field extraction: construct gauge-invariant dressed variables, distinguish dressing from gauge fixing, and rewrite electroweak fields using the Higgs field. They still assume Standard Model electroweak structure and do not supply a GU-native dressing field, observed-sector vacuum, electroweak embedding, separate W/Z source rows, weak angle/coupling lineage, Higgs scalar-source/self-coupling lineage, pole extraction, mass scale, or GeV normalization.
nextRequiredArtifact: A GU-native dressing field or gauge-invariant composite-variable theorem derived from Shiab/observer-sector geometry.
A source-derived GU observed vacuum and electroweak embedding producing photon/W/Z/H projection rows before target comparison.
Separate W and Z source rows with target-independent weak-angle, coupling, scale, and stability sidecars.
A Higgs scalar-source/self-coupling lineage plus physical-pole extraction and GeV normalization before any dressing-field route can promote W/Z/H masses.
